_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised autopilot, LOCAL track)
_meta.note: FL Tampa locals batch — Mayor Castor + Council D2 Maniscalco, D3 Hurtak, D5 Young, D6 Miranda. Plus status-fix on D7 Viera (RESIGNED 2025-11-02 to run for FL HD-67). Roster verified June 2026 against tampa.gov + Ballotpedia. Scores reset to null where no evidence; TRUE/FALSE only where a documented vote, statement, or endorsement exists. Surfacing high-value deviations: Castor's former-police-chief pro-TPD posture; Miranda's heterodox old-school fiscal-conservative profile (lone NO on 2009 trans-HRO, NO on millage, NO on marijuana decrim); Maniscalco + Hurtak as part of the 4-3 anti-millage bloc.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of formal church membership, denomination, or attendance; issued generic 'love thy neighbor' Easter civic message as mayor
profile.education: BA Criminology, University of Tampa (basketball/volleyball scholarship; UT Hall of Famer); MPA, Troy State University; Chamberlain HS, Tampa
profile.background: Mayor of Tampa, sworn May 1, 2019; re-elected March 7, 2023, ~73% in runoff; term to 2027. Tampa mayoral races officially non-partisan; switched R to D in 2015. Prior: Tampa Police Dept. 1984-2015 (31 yrs); first female Tampa Police Chief 2009-2015. Married Ana Cruz (lobbyist; managing partner Ballard Partners; daughter of former state Sen. Janet Cruz) 2011. Two adopted sons from Ukraine. First openly LGBT mayor of Tampa.

jane-castor.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Tampa Mayor since May 2019, re-elected 2023 with ~73% runoff vote, term to 2027 (tampa.gov, Ballotpedia). HARD EVIDENCE record: (1) sanctity_of_life — directed TPD will NOT arrest abortion-providers/seekers Aug 2022, '100% not going to happen' (Axios); called abortion access 'a fundamental right' (WUSF). (2) biblical_marriage — first openly LGBT Tampa mayor, married Ana Cruz 2011 (CNN/HRC); raises Pride flag annually at Old City Hall (tampa.gov 2026, LA Blade 2024); twice Tampa Pride grand marshal; explicitly criticized 'Don't Say Gay' law as bigotry (Florida Politics). (3) economic_stewardship — Sept 2023 proposed 16% property-tax/millage hike (7.2076 mills, highest in decades); council rejected 4-3 after 7-hour hearing (Tampa Bay Times, Fox 13). (4) public_justice — as former TPD Chief publicly refused defund, *increased* TPD budget by $13M in 2020 over protester objections, called TPD 'the best police department in the United States' (Tampa Bay Times, Creative Loafing). DEVIATION-FLAG: Castor's pro-police posture is genuine and unusual for a left-Dem mayor — score TRUE on public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR met via affirmative advocacy + $13M budget increase against pressure). (5) refuse_state_overreach q2 — issued outdoor mask EO Jan 2021 around Super Bowl LV with $500 fines; photographed maskless herself at the game (ESPN, Newsweek fact-check) — opposite of 'protected citizens from mandates.' (6) border_immigration — March 2026 AG Uthmeier formally found TPD policies were illegal sanctuary under FL law; Castor publicly defended posture before revising under removal threat (Florida Politics, The Hill, US Conf of Mayors quote: 'fundamental reason local law enforcement should not be engaged in immigration enforcement'). (7) self_defense — co-chair Mayors Against Illegal Guns / Everytown; endorsed red-flag laws explicitly ('keep guns out of the hands of people who should not have them'); called for 'stricter gun regulations' after Ybor City shooting (Everytown, Creative Loafing). Treats gun violence as public-health issue at Biden White House summit (Everytown, WTSP) → public_justice q3 FALSE. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on school choice, parental notification, voter ID / paper ballots / Zuckerbucks, cashless bail, victim restitution, refuse-state-overreach (q0/q1/q3/q4 — her pushback against FL state directives is directionally LEFTWARD, so reads null on the conservative-rubric framing per the LOCAL skill discipline). christian_liberty cells all null — no public profession of Christ documented.

0.note: Publicly stated Roe overturn 'will remove protections' and called abortion access 'a fundamental right' — explicit pro-choice framing, opposite of personhood-at-conception.

1.note: Aug 19, 2022 directed TPD will NOT arrest those who obtain or perform illegal abortions; '100% not going to happen' — anti-abolition posture.

0.note: Tampa's first openly LGBTQ mayor; married partner Ana Cruz 2011; HRC-backed campaign.

2.note: At annual Pride flag raisings explicitly affirms transgender Tampans as community; raises Pride flag at Old City Hall annually under her administration.

4.note: Publicly criticized FL Parental Rights in Education Act ('Don't Say Gay') and committed Tampa to 'do everything I can legislatively' to protect LGBTQ rights.

2.note: Opposed Florida's Parental Rights in Education Act framework — opposite of opposing SOGI/gender-ideology content in K-12.
Sept 2023 proposed a 16% property-tax / millage hike (to 7.2076 mills, highest in decades); Tampa City Council rejected 4-3 after a 7-hour hearing.

0.note: Former Tampa Police Chief (2009-2015); publicly refused to defund TPD in 2020, INCREASED TPD budget by $13M over protester objections, called TPD 'the best police department in the United States' — affirmative advocacy meeting HIGH BAR.

3.note: Co-chair Mayors Against Illegal Guns / Everytown affiliated; treats gun violence as a public-health issue at Biden White House gun-legislation summit.

2.note: Issued outdoor mask mandate executive order Jan 30, 2021 around Super Bowl LV with $500 fines for non-compliance — imposed mandate, opposite of 'protected citizens from mandates.'

1.note: US Conference of Mayors public stance she signed onto: 'fundamental reason local law enforcement should not be engaged in immigration enforcement' — opposite of supporting mandatory deportation cooperation.

2.note: March 2026 FL AG Uthmeier formally found TPD policies constituted illegal sanctuary under Florida law; Castor publicly defended the policy before revising under threat of removal.

0.note: Called for 'stricter gun regulations' after Ybor City shooting — opposite of constitutional carry.

1.note: Endorsed red-flag laws explicitly: said they 'will help keep guns out of the hands of people who should not have them.' Everytown summit affiliation.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of specific church affiliation; Cuban-American Ybor City origin (father emigrated from Cuba 1911) makes nominal Roman Catholic background plausible but uncited
profile.education: BA Criminology (minors Spanish/Sociology), University of Tampa 1977; AA/AS Hillsborough Community College; Adult Business School; Thomas Jefferson HS (Tampa) 1956-59
profile.background: Tampa City Council, District 6 (geographic seat, West Tampa / Seminole Heights / South Tampa), sworn May 1, 2023 after Apr 25, 2023 runoff (51% vs. atty. Hoyt Prindle). Term to May 1, 2027. 9th non-consecutive term (1974-79, 1995-2003, 2007-15, 2015-23 D2, 2023-27 D6). 'Dean of Tampa politics' / 'old school.' Career: State Steward FL Thoroughbred Horse Racing (1988-); racehorse breeder/trainer (1979-88); managing partner Cafe Pepe Inc. restaurant Tampa (1980, 1986-). Wife referenced; suffered two miscarriages per his own 2022 council disclosure.

charlie-miranda.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: District 6 Tampa, sworn May 1, 2023, term to May 1, 2027; 9th non-consecutive term (Ballotpedia, tampa.gov). HIGH-VALUE DEVIATION: Miranda is the council's heterodox old-school fiscal-conservative Democrat. HARD evidence: (1) 2009 LONE-DISSENT NO vote (5-1) on adding 'gender identity' to Tampa Human Rights Ordinance, citing concerns about 'people who come to work one day dressed as one gender, and the next day as the other' (Bilerico/LGBTQ Nation, WMNF) — biblical_marriage q2 TRUE (rejects trans ideology). (2) Sept 2023 NO vote (4-3) on Mayor Castor's 16% / 1-mill property-tax hike — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE (WTSP, Fox 13). (3) Affirmative pro-police record — on-record 2020 pledge to 'quit a year into his eighth term' if Mayor Castor fired Chief Dugan; consistent 'fully staffed' rhetoric over multi-decade career (Florida Politics) — public_justice q0 TRUE, HIGH BAR met via personal affirmative advocacy. (4) 2016 LONE-DISSENT NO vote (6-1) on Tampa marijuana decriminalization, framed as 'back-dooring legalization' (WFLA) and tied teen-crime to drug deals rather than social-services framing — public_justice q3 TRUE (refuses public-health frame). (5) Voted (with Citro, Maniscalco, Viera) AGAINST public vote on giving Citizen Review Board independent subpoena power (CL Tampa). LIMIT: Voted YES (5-2 passage) on June 2022 Tampa abortion-rights resolution opposing criminalization — sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE (CL Tampa, WTSP). Shared personal miscarriage testimony but ultimately voted with the council majority for the pro-choice resolution. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on personhood (q0), school choice/parental-notification/SOGI, christian_liberty cells, election integrity (no council jurisdiction), cashless bail, victim restitution, refuse-state-overreach cells, immigration (no council vote located on 287(g) sanctuary question), or self-defense (FL preempts municipal firearms) — those cells null, not penalized.

1.note: Voted YES (5-2 passage) on June 2022 Tampa City Council resolution opposing criminalization of abortion — pro-choice posture, opposite of abolition advocacy.

2.note: 2009 LONE-DISSENT NO vote (5-1) on adding 'gender identity' to Tampa Human Rights Ordinance; raised concerns about 'people who come to work one day dressed as one gender, and the next day as the other' — explicit rejection of trans-identity policy framework.
Sept 2023 voted NO on Mayor Castor's 16% / 1-mill property-tax hike; council killed it 4-3 (Carlson, Hurtak, Maniscalco, Miranda). Self-described 'fiscal conservative.'

0.note: Affirmative pro-police advocacy: publicly vowed in 2020 he'd 'quit a year into his eighth term' if Mayor Castor fired Chief Brian Dugan; consistent 'fully staffed' Police/Fire rhetoric across multi-decade career.

3.note: 2016 LONE-DISSENT NO vote (6-1) on Tampa marijuana decriminalization: 'we're back-dooring legalization of something' — explicit rejection of social-services / public-health framing for drug crime.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of current parish or public faith profession; attended Tampa Catholic High School (background only)
profile.education: BA English, University of South Florida; AA, Hillsborough Community College; Tampa Catholic High School
profile.background: Tampa City Council, District 2 (citywide at-large), sworn May 1, 2023, term to April 30, 2027. 4th cumulative council term overall (D6: 2014-2023, three terms; D2 at-large: 2023-). Council Chair May 2020-May 2021 and May 2023-May 2025 (removed mid-term as chair in 2025 after transparency dispute over a mayoral letter; replaced by Clendenin). Born/raised in Tampa, son of Cuban and Italian immigrants from West Tampa. Long civic-board resume: Hillsborough River Interlocal Planning Board (chair), Tampa Theatre, Straz Center, Tampa Museum of Art, Public Arts Committee, Hillsborough County Human Rights Council; MacFarlane Park Neighborhood Assoc. president 2013-15.

guido-maniscalco.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: District 2 at-large Tampa, sworn May 1, 2023, term to April 30, 2027 (tampa.gov, Ballotpedia). Long civic-arts board service. HARD evidence: (1) Sept 2023 NO vote on Mayor Castor's 16% millage hike; one of the 4-3 majority that killed it (Carlson, Hurtak, Maniscalco, Miranda). Quoted: 'Now is not the time to do this... We have to get creative. We have to dig deeper, and we can't just say raise the millage. That's the easy way out.' (WTSP, Tampa Bay Times) — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE. (2) Voted YES (5-2 passage) on June 2022 Tampa abortion-rights resolution opposing criminalization (CL Tampa) — sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE. (3) As Council Chair (2023-25) presided over Tampa's annual Pride Day proclamations and the city's Pride flag-raising ceremony at Old City Hall (tampa.gov Pride Day proclamation; tampa.gov 2025 LGBT flag-raising release); sits on Hillsborough County Human Rights Council — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE (presiding over LGBTQ+ policy ceremonies as Chair without dissent + HRC membership). HEAVILY MIXED on police: 2020 public statement 'majority support for the police department... People are telling me constantly: Don't cut the police. We want to feel safe' — but ALSO marched with protesters and called for TPD to sell off military-surplus gear; this fails the HIGH BAR for affirmative advocacy — public_justice q0 null. Voted (with Citro, Miranda, Viera) AGAINST public vote on CRB subpoena power. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on personhood, marriage definition / trans ideology specifically, school choice / parental notification / SOGI, christian_liberty cells, election integrity, cashless bail, victim restitution, refuse-state-overreach cells, border/immigration (Tampa is on FL Phoenix list of cities that as of mid-2025 had NOT signed 287(g) MOAs, but Maniscalco has not publicly pushed for or against — null), self-defense (FL preempts) — those cells null.

1.note: Voted YES (5-2 passage) on June 2022 Tampa City Council resolution opposing criminalization of abortion.

4.note: As Council Chair (2020-21, 2023-25) presided over Tampa's annual Pride Day proclamations and city Pride flag-raising at Old City Hall; sits on Hillsborough County Human Rights Council per his official Tampa.gov council bio.

2.note: Sept 2023 voted NO on Mayor Castor's 16% millage hike — the deciding-bloc vote (4-3 against). Quoted: 'Now is not the time to do this... raise the millage. That's the easy way out.'

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of church affiliation or denomination
profile.background: Amanda Lynn Hurtak (b. 1977, Tampa). Tampa City Council, District 3 (citywide at-large). Appointed April 7, 2022 (succeeded John Dingfelder, 4-2 council vote); won runoff April 25, 2023 vs. Janet Cruz. Term to May 1, 2027. Filed Feb 23, 2026 to run for Tampa mayor in 2027. Former Idylwild Elementary (Gainesville) teacher; works as technical editor on West Africa aid programs. Old Seminole Heights Neighborhood Assn. trustee; Variance Review Board; Charter Review Commission. Husband: media-consultant/journalist Tim Burke (subject of 2023 FBI raid).

lynn-hurtak.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: District 3 at-large Tampa Council, appointed April 2022, re-elected April 2023 runoff vs. Janet Cruz, term to May 2027. Filed Feb 23, 2026 to run for Tampa mayor 2027 (Florida Politics, Ballotpedia). HARD evidence: (1) sanctity_of_life — initiated and co-wrote 2022 Tampa abortion-rights resolution alongside Tampa Democratic Socialists of America; her version was actually MORE aggressive (sought to strip funding from crisis pregnancy centers, deprioritize enforcement) — voted AGAINST the final softened version because it wasn't strong enough (WUSF, Florida Politics on DSA ties). Mayoral platform 'Protecting Women, Doctors' highlights reproductive rights as central plank (lynnhurtak.com) — sanctity_of_life q0/q1 FALSE. (2) biblical_marriage — campaign endorsed by Florida LGBTQ+ Democratic Caucus and Hillsborough County LGBTQ+ Democratic Caucus (lynnhurtak.com/endorsements) — HARD evidence on LGBTQ-affirming posture; q0 FALSE, q4 FALSE. (3) economic_stewardship — Sept 2023 NO vote (4-3) on Mayor Castor's 16% millage hike (WTSP); voted against council's own $20K member raise (WFLA) — q2 TRUE. DEVIATION-FLAG: Hurtak is genuinely the council's executive-skeptic / budget-skeptic — Republicans publicly aligned with her against Cruz in the 2023 runoff (Florida Politics). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on personhood specifically as religious framing (her cell q0 score reflects her abortion-rights advocacy), trans ideology cell (no direct trans-specific statement), school choice / parental notification / SOGI K-12 (not council jurisdiction; no resolutions located), christian_liberty cells (no Christian profession; DSA-collaboration weights against alignment but not enough for false on q0-q4 without direct anti-Christian statements — null), election integrity (council no jurisdiction), public_justice cells beyond q0 (q0 null because her police-skepticism is documented via CRB-subpoena-power push and DSA-aligned activist backing but no direct anti-police vote located beyond CRB push), cashless bail, refuse-state-overreach (her anti-state-preemption posture is directionally LEFTWARD; null on conservative rubric), immigration, self-defense (council no jurisdiction).

0.note: Initiated 2022 Tampa abortion-protection resolution post-Dobbs; mayoral platform 'Protecting Women, Doctors' centers reproductive rights — explicit anti-personhood framing.

1.note: Co-wrote the 2022 abortion-rights resolution with Tampa DSA; voted against final softened version because it wasn't aggressive enough on deprioritizing enforcement and stripping CPC funding — opposite of abolition.

0.note: Campaign endorsed by Florida LGBTQ+ Democratic Caucus and Hillsborough County LGBTQ+ Democratic Caucus — explicit pro-LGBTQ platform alignment, opposite of one-man/one-woman marriage.

4.note: Florida LGBTQ+ Democratic Caucus and Hillsborough County LGBTQ+ Democratic Caucus endorsements; council voted with majority for annual Pride flag-raising at Old City Hall.

2.note: Sept 2023 voted NO on Mayor Castor's 16% millage hike (4-3 majority that killed it); also voted against council's own $20K member raise.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of personal church affiliation; her family nonprofit (Tampa Heights Junior Civic Association) is renovating the historic 'Old Faith Temple Church' as a community/civic project, not as a worship body
profile.education: MA Political Science, Howard University; BA Culture & Media Communication, University of South Florida; AA, Hillsborough Community College; Nonprofit Management Certificate, University of Tampa; Middleton HS / Brandon HS (Tampa); TOBA Leadership Institute alum
profile.background: Tampa City Council, District 5 (geographic seat, East Tampa / Tampa Heights). Won runoff Oct 28, 2025, 61-39% vs. former councilman Thomas Scott, in special election to serve remainder of late Gwen Henderson's term (Henderson died June 2025). Term to 2027. Age 33; raised in Tampa. Executive Director of Tampa Heights Junior Civic Association (founded by her grandmother Lena Young-Green). First-time elected official. Chairs Council Neighborhood & Community Affairs; serves on Public Safety Coordinating Council. NOTE: she did NOT succeed Orlando Gudes directly — Gudes lost the March 2023 D5 race to Gwen Henderson (~80-vote margin); Young succeeded Henderson after Henderson's death.

naya-young.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: District 5 Tampa, won Oct 28, 2025 runoff 61-39% in special election, serving remainder of Gwen Henderson's term (Henderson died June 2025). First-time officeholder, seated ~8 months. Chairs Council Neighborhood & Community Affairs; on Public Safety Coordinating Council (WUSF, Tampa Monitor, Axios). HARD evidence from endorsements (the LOCAL skill's social-media protocol explicitly accepts single-issue-PAC endorsements as HARD): campaign formally endorsed by Florida Rising (left-progressive issue org), Hillsborough County LGBTQ+ Democratic Caucus, and listed by Tampa Bay LGBT Chamber as LGBTQ-friendly elected official — biblical_marriage q0 FALSE and q4 FALSE. nayafortampa.com endorsements page also includes Ruth's List Florida (dedicated to electing Democratic women in support of abortion rights) — sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on most other cells: no full FY budget vote yet (seated post-FY26 cycle); no documented direct statements on abortion personhood, trans ideology specifically, school choice, parental notification, CRT/SOGI K-12, christian_liberty cells, election integrity, public-justice cells (her bio cites 'community policing' and 'law-enforcement reform' background work in DC — reform/oversight framing rather than affirmative pro-police, but no Tampa vote yet to score q0), cashless bail, refuse-state-overreach, border/immigration, or self-defense. Most cells null (not penalized) — confidence evidence_local set on the documented endorsement-based cells.

1.note: Campaign endorsed by Ruth's List Florida — PAC dedicated to electing pro-abortion-rights Democratic women — explicit endorsement signaling opposite of abolition advocacy.

0.note: Endorsed by Hillsborough County LGBTQ+ Democratic Caucus; listed by Tampa Bay LGBT Chamber as LGBTQ-friendly elected official.

set.office: City Council, District 7 (RESIGNED 2025-11-02 to run for FL HD-67)

profile.religion: Son of Cuban refugees; SOFT inference of Catholic background unconfirmed in public record — pluralist public framing ('no matter what God they worship') is the documented stance
profile.education: JD, Stetson College of Law (2003); BA, University of South Florida (2000); FL Bar 2003, certified mediator
profile.background: Tampa City Council, District 7 (New Tampa / Tampa Palms / Fowler-Busch corridor) Dec 8, 2016 — RESIGNED Nov 2, 2025 to run for Florida House District 67. Term would have expired May 1, 2027. Council Chair 2019-2020; Public Safety Committee Chair; HART (transit) Chair 2023-26. Civil litigation attorney (insurance defense, premises, auto) at Banker Lopez Gassler P.A.; Super Lawyers honoree 2015-21, 2024. Older brother Juan has autism — driver of his signature disability-rights work (Tampa All-Abilities Playground, $1.7M FY21 budget secure; Karen Jacobs Award 2023; founder/former president Lawyers Autism Awareness Foundation; former board Tampa Bay Best Buddies).

luis-viera.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement — STATUS FIX] Luis Viera RESIGNED his Tampa City Council District 7 seat effective Nov 2, 2025 to run for Florida House District 67 (Florida Politics, Bay News 9, Ballotpedia). Term would have run to May 1, 2027. Set status=former, candidacy_status=running_higher_office; he exits the active local queue. Historical (2016-2025) record below is preserved on the LOCAL rubric where evidence is documented; if he later wins HD-67 a state-tier re-score is warranted. HARD evidence from his 9-year council tenure: (1) sanctity_of_life — voted YES on Aug 2022 Tampa abortion-rights resolution; later opposed Hurtak's harder charter-amendment version (CL Tampa) — q1 FALSE. (2) biblical_marriage — Tampa earned perfect HRC Municipal Equality Index score during his tenure; listed by Tampa Bay LGBT Chamber as LGBTQ-friendly elected; his campaign site (vieraforflorida.com) uses pluralist 'no matter who they love' framing — q0 FALSE on his published stance, q4 FALSE on aggregate council LGBTQ posture. (3) public_justice — Chair of Council Public Safety Committee; voted (with Citro, Miranda, Maniscalco) to BLOCK public vote on Citizen Review Board subpoena power (CL Tampa Aug 2022); voted to renew ShotSpotter/SoundThinking contract; later motioned to dissolve CRB to comply with FL state law (Fox 13). Pro-police-oversight-deferral, pro-Chief, pro-TPD-tooling pattern — public_justice q0 TRUE, HIGH BAR met via affirmative committee-chair advocacy. (4) economic_stewardship — voted NO on Castor's 16% millage in Sept 2023 (6-1 against Castor's full ask) but motioned an alternative smaller hike for public safety — MIXED; q2 TRUE on the millage opposition (the rejected-hike cell, consistent with Carlson/Hurtak/Maniscalco/Miranda dissent). (5) border_immigration — introduced 2021 Tampa Council resolution welcoming Afghan refugees, framed as 'voice for all refugees'; son of Cuban refugees (Tampa Bay Times) — q1 FALSE (anti-mandatory-deportation framing). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on personhood, marriage definition, trans-specific votes, school choice / parental notification / SOGI K-12, christian_liberty cells, election integrity, cashless bail, victim restitution, refuse-state-overreach cells (he COMPLIED with state CRB-dissolution mandate, not refused), or self-defense — those cells null. Disability/autism advocacy is signature work but doesn't map cleanly to any rubric cell.

1.note: Voted YES (5-2 passage) on June 2022 Tampa City Council abortion-rights resolution opposing criminalization.

0.note: Campaign site uses pluralist 'no matter who they love' framing; listed by Tampa Bay LGBT Chamber as LGBTQ-friendly elected.

4.note: Listed by Tampa Bay LGBT Chamber as LGBTQ-friendly elected; Tampa MEI perfect score during his tenure (no documented dissent).

2.note: Sept 2023 voted NO on Mayor Castor's 16% millage hike; was part of the 4-3 (or 6-1 on full Castor ask) opposition to the largest property-tax increase in Tampa decades.

0.note: Chair Council Public Safety Committee; voted to block public vote on giving Citizen Review Board independent subpoena power (with Citro, Miranda, Maniscalco); voted to renew ShotSpotter; motioned dissolution of CRB to comply with FL state law — affirmative pro-TPD-chief / pro-tooling pattern.

1.note: Introduced 2021 Tampa Council resolution welcoming Afghan refugees, framed as 'voice for all refugees' — anti-mandatory-deportation posture.
